Fix 6-player finale: sort rounds not individual table matches

matches per-table independently broke this — a player could appear
in round 1 on two tables. Now sorts entire rounds (3 matches together),
moving rounds with same-club matches earlier.

commit.message:
fix: add /opt/unitree_robotics/lib to ENV LD_LIBRARY_PATH + remove --no-banner from tests

The Docker ENV LD_LIBRARY_PATH had /cyclonedds/install/lib before
/opt/unitree_robotics/lib, causing g1_ctrl to load mismatched DDS libs.
Adding unitree path first ensures both ddsc + ddscxx resolve from same build.
Also removed --no-banner from conda run in tests (unsupported flag).

commit.message:
security: Add base-uri and object-src directives to CSP

Adds `base-uri 'self'` to prevent base tag injection attacks and
`object-src 'none'` to block plugin (Flash/Java applet) injection,
completing the CSP to align with best practices.

feat(cache): Lookup Cache Phase 2 — IReadOnlyList, ConnectionKey, global TenantIsolation, helper methods (Closes #142)

- Change GetAll/GetAllAsync return type from List<T> to IReadOnlyList<T> to prevent accidental cache mutation
- Add ConnectionKey property to [CacheLookup] for multi-database support (e.g. ORSS read-only DB)
- Change TenantIsolation to three-state (unset/true/false) with global default via LookupCacheOptions
- Add GetLookupItem<T> for single-item lookup by predicate
- Add GetLookupSelectList<T> for dropdown integration (ComboSelectListItem)
- Add GetAttribute and DefaultTenantIsolation to ILookupCacheService
- Update CacheLookupAttribute remarks to reference FrameworkContext.SaveChanges
- Update docs with new features, stampede protection note, filter semantics, EmptyContext caveat
- Add 7 new tests covering IReadOnlyList, GetAttribute, DefaultTenantIsolation, ConnectionKey

commit.message:
revert: switch back to isnet_fp16 - quint8 caused quality degradation

- isnet_quint8 caused transparent gaps at person/table boundaries
- isnet_fp16 is safe on mobile because depth model is disposed before
  IS-Net loads → peak memory ~84MB (never both models simultaneously)

Remove exposed credentials from PLAN.md and harden .gitignore

Replaces hardcoded database host and password in PLAN.md with
placeholder variables. Adds dashboard/.env.local, .vercel/,
node_modules/, and .next/ to .gitignore to prevent accidental
future commits of secrets.

Note: old password was rotated in Supabase after exposure.

feat: add skip options for personality/embedding prompts in setup.sh --force

When running --force on an existing installation, ask "Change personality
settings? (y/N)" and "Change embedding/RAG settings? (y/N)" before showing
the full prompt blocks. Default is No, so users can skip through quickly.

Also fixes custom persona preservation: loads existing persona from DB,

avoids double-wrapping when kept.

commit.message:
feat(cli): add --oauth-port option for configurable OAuth callback port (#21016)

* feat(cli): add --oauth-port option for configurable OAuth callback port

Allow users to specify a fixed port for the local OAuth callback server
via --oauth-port flag or LIGHTDASH_OAUTH_PORT env var. This is useful in
VS Code devcontainers where random ports may not be auto-forwarded.

Closes #21013

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cli): validate oauth port is a number between 1 and 65535

Addresses PR review: parseInt returns NaN for invalid input which would
pass through as truthy and cause server.listen(NaN) to fail.

commit.message:
fix: permitir crear remito después de facturar cotización

Factura y remito son independientes — antes, al facturar (o generar
remito), el status cambiaba a CONVERTED y bloqueaba la otra acción.

Backend:
- generateDeliveryNoteFromQuote() acepta status ACCEPTED y CONVERTED
- Ya no cambia status a CONVERTED al generar remito (solo registra
  en historial). El status cambia a CONVERTED solo al enviar factura
  real a Colppy.

Frontend (vista detalle cotización):
- CONVERTED muestra "Generar Remito" si no tiene remito aún
- Muestra "Ver Remito" si ya tiene uno
- Badge "Factura enviada a Colppy" si fue sincronizada

commit.message:
fix: switch IS-Net to quint8 model for mobile memory stability

- isnet_fp16 (84MB) → isnet_quint8 (~42MB): halves person model memory
- Used only as binary boost layer (alpha > 30 → 255), so quantization
  has negligible impact on final output quality
- Combined with depth model disposal, total peak memory now ~42MB vs ~111MB

fix: dispose depth model before loading IS-Net to prevent mobile OOM

- Load depth model → run inference → dispose before person segmentation
- Only one AI model in WASM memory at a time (prevents mobile OOM)
- Model files stay in browser Cache API → fast reload from cache
- Remove global depthEstimator singleton (create fresh each call)
- Cache detectDevice() result to avoid repeated WebGPU probing
- All processing parameters unchanged

commit.message:
fix: g1_ctrl CycloneDDS ABI crash + video2robot missing conda envs

g1_ctrl crashed with "free(): invalid pointer" due to CycloneDDS ABI
mismatch: libddsc.so loaded from standalone build (/cyclonedds/install)
while libddscxx.so loaded from unitree_sdk2 (/opt/unitree_robotics).
Fix: remove wrong include paths from CMakeLists, replace /cyclonedds/install/lib
with /opt/unitree_robotics/lib in LD_LIBRARY_PATH.

video2robot pipeline failed with EnvironmentLocationNotFound for phmr/gmr.
Fix: clone with --recursive for submodules, create phmr + gmr conda envs.

commit.message:
Backend cleanup + comprehensive edge-case hardening

Backend:
- TTS + STT: wrap Sarvam SDK calls in asyncio.wait_for(timeout=30s) via
  run_in_executor so slow/hung API calls surface as 504 instead of hanging
  the worker forever; user-friendly 504 message shown on timeout
- Both routes: extract human-readable message from verbose SDK exceptions
  using the same regex pattern (no raw stack traces exposed)
- Remove unused /translate route from main.py (translate.py kept but
  unregistered — dead code no longer reachable)

Frontend fetch timeouts (api/sarvam.ts):
- Added timeoutSignal() helper (AbortController + setTimeout)
- TTS and STT fetch calls both carry a 35s AbortSignal — slightly above the
  backend's 30s so the backend error message reaches the client cleanly

SpeakRepeat edge cases:
- Max recording duration: auto-stops MediaRecorder after 60s so user can
  never be stuck holding the mic button
- STT processing timeout: Promise.race() against a 40s reject so the
  "analysing…" spinner cannot loop forever on a Sarvam API hang
- Error state: replaced bare red text with a dismissible card containing
  a "Try again" button that clears the error and re-enables recording

ListenIdentify edge cases:
- TTS failure: replaced undismissable red text with a banner card that
  includes a "Skip →" button calling onResult(false) — user is never
  permanently blocked when audio fails to load

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(cache): replace LookupInvalidationInterceptor with FrameworkContext.SaveChanges override

Cache invalidation is now automatic via property injection on FrameworkContext,
requiring zero Program.cs setup. LookupInvalidationInterceptor and its manual
AddInterceptors() step are removed.

- Add ILookupCacheService? property to FrameworkContext
- Override all 4 SaveChanges/SaveChangesAsync overloads to collect dirty
  [CacheLookup] types before base call, invalidate after
- WTMContext.DC getter injects LookupCacheService via property injection
- Remove LookupInvalidationInterceptor.cs and its DI registration
- Fix FrameworkContextInvalidationTests: override OnModelCreating in
  TestFwContext to skip Utils.GetAllModels() assembly scan (fixes
  SQLite "duplicate column name: MajorId" in test env)
- Update docs/lookup-cache.md: remove AddInterceptors step entirely

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Replace type-translation with select-phrase MCQ, fix STT errors + audio format

SelectPhrase exercise (new):
- English prompt shown in card ("which phrase means '...'")
- 4 target-language script options as tappable buttons
- Correct answer highlighted in indigo, wrong in terracotta, others dimmed
- Inline feedback shows target text + romanized pronunciation after any tap
- No TTS or keyboard needed — fully offline, instant

Hindi lessons — all 12 type-translation exercises converted to select-phrase:
- Each exercise gains an options[] array of 4 plausible Hindi distractors
- Correct answer is always exercise.targetText; distractors are other phrases
  from the same unit to stay contextually relevant

STT error display:
- Backend now parses the Sarvam SDK exception and extracts only the
  human-readable 'message' field — never exposes raw headers/body
- SpeakRepeat shows a fixed friendly string on any STT failure, not the
  raw Error.message (which could contain the full API response)

Audio format (iOS fix):
- MediaRecorder now checks supported types in priority order:
  audio/webm;codecs=opus → audio/ogg;codecs=opus → audio/mp4 → audio/webm
- The actual extension (webm/ogg/mp4) is derived from the chosen MIME type
  and passed to the stt() API call so the backend filename is always correct
- Previously always sent 'recording.webm' even on iOS where the data is mp4,
  causing Sarvam to reject it with "Failed to read the file"

Viewport scroll fix:
- Exercise.tsx adds a scrollRef on the content div and resets scrollTop to 0
  on every currentIdx change — prevents content starting above the viewport

Exercise.tsx:
- Swaps TypeTranslation import for SelectPhrase, wires the new render case

commit.message:
fix: use CPU for person segmentation to fix mobile crash

The @imgly/background-removal device was hardcoded to 'gpu' which
crashes on many mobile devices. Changed to 'cpu' (WASM) which is
universally supported. IS-Net fp16 is small enough to run fast on CPU.

The depth model already had proper GPU detection with WASM fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix position add-on handling: blended entry price, weighted hold time

When adding to an existing position:
- Recalculate thesis entryPrice as share-weighted average across all buys
- Recalculate stopPrice (ATR-based) and targetPrice (fib-based) from blended cost
- Update peakPrice immediately if add-on price exceeds current peak

Fix manual sell path to use share-weighted hold time (matching legacy AI path)
instead of simple sellDate - firstBuyDate.

cleanup on disconnect.

fix: remove white fringe around people edges

Person mask edge pixels (IS-Net alpha 30-128) that have wall-like
color are no longer boosted to full opacity. This removes the white
halo/fringe visible at person boundaries.

- Edge pixels on wall-colored area: NOT boosted (white fringe removed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix audio error flash, optimize STT speed, sort exercises, add skip button

Audio error handling (both ListenIdentify + SpeakRepeat):
- Replace 1-second display buffer with a silent retry after 1.5 s
- Keeps the loading spinner visible during retry — no error flash if the
  backend was just cold-starting; error only surfaces if retry also fails

STT latency on mobile (SpeakRepeat):
- Prefer audio/webm;codecs=opus over plain webm (better compression)
- Cap audioBitsPerSecond at 24000 (24 kbps) — adequate for speech STT,
  ~5-8x smaller than the default 128 kbps, significantly faster to upload

Exercise order (Exercise.tsx):
- Sort exercises by type at render time: listen-identify → type-translation
  → speak-repeat, so quick tapping exercises come first and voice recording
  is deferred to the end of each lesson

Skip control (Exercise.tsx):
- Add a lightweight "skip →" button in the lesson header row
- Advances to the next question without completing the current one
- If used on the last exercise, goes to lesson complete without awarding XP
  (skipped lesson = incomplete)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(cache): add [CacheLookup] attribute for static/lookup table caching (Closes #139)

Adds a zero-friction, attribute-driven cache layer for low-churn reference
tables (city codes, status dicts, etc.).  A single attribute eliminates
repeated DB round-trips for data that rarely changes.

Key components:
- [CacheLookup(TtlMinutes, TenantIsolation, WarmOnStartup)] attribute
- ILookupCacheService / LookupCacheService backed by IMemoryCache
  - per-type CancellationTokenSource enables cross-tenant batch invalidation
  - CTS disposed outside lock to avoid ObjectDisposedException race
- LookupInvalidationInterceptor (EF Core SaveChangesInterceptor)
  - auto-invalidates on any write; requires opt-in AddInterceptors() in app
- LookupCacheWarmupService (BackgroundService)
  - warms WarmOnStartup=true types 3s after startup; failures are warnings only
- WTMContext.GetLookup<T>(predicate?) / GetLookupAsync<T>()
  - in-memory predicate filter avoids extra DB queries
- 21 MSTest tests covering hit/miss, invalidation, tenant isolation,
  interceptor, warm-up type filtering, and attribute defaults
- docs/lookup-cache.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: depth-weighted color detection to protect close objects (table)

Color detection now considers depth: close objects (table/food) resist
color-based removal using farness = pow(1 - depth/255, 1.5).

- White tablecloth (close, depth=200): farness≈0.10 → mostly opaque
- White wall (far, depth=50): farness≈0.72 → removed
- Grey floor (far, depth=80): farness≈0.57 → removed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

debug(x-chat): add debug endpoint with screenshot + DOM dump

GET /api/orchestrator/x-chat/debug   → JSON with testIds, chatHtml, url, title
GET /api/orchestrator/x-chat/debug?screenshot=1  → PNG of current page

Lets us see exactly what Puppeteer is seeing to diagnose selector issues.

commit.message:
Improve phrase quality across all languages + sharpen pronunciation feedback

Lesson data (Tamil, Telugu, Bengali, Marathi):
- Unit title changed from 'Greetings & Basics' to 'Everyday Conversations'
- Lesson 1 'Meeting Someone': isolated 'Hello' → combined 'Hello, how are you?';
  bare 'Thank you' → richer 'Thank you so much/very much'; added natural follow-up
  questions (name, 'I'm fine and you?')
- Lesson 2 'Everyday Replies': single-word yes/no → contextual phrases
  ('Yes, alright', 'No thanks', 'Sure go ahead', 'Sorry I don't know',
  'No problem at all')
- Lesson 3 'Getting Around': replaced 'Come here' with 'I don't know [Language]'
  — the most practically useful learner phrase

SpeakRepeat pronunciation feedback (PRD — Messaging Clarity):
- qualitativeFeedback now takes romanized instead of englishText, so notes
  reference the target pronunciation directly (e.g. 'say "Namaskara" slowly')
- Notes are more specific: medium scores name the romanized phrase/first chunk
- Result card now shows a two-row comparison grid: target romanized vs
  'you said' transcript — gives learner a clear visual diff to self-correct

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
